expectations.text: The SQL injection via f-string interpolation of req.query_params is identified as a finding
expectations.evidence: Second High finding: '`since` query parameter is interpolated into the SQL via an f-string, producing a textbook SQL injection sink' at api/routes/admin.py:11

expectations.text: The verb in URL (`/listAllUsers`) is identified — should be `GET /admin/users`
expectations.evidence: Medium finding: 'URL uses a verb (`/admin/listAllUsers`) instead of a resource. ... Rename to `GET /admin/users`'

expectations.text: The missing pagination is identified (design says 'paginated')
expectations.evidence: Medium finding: 'No pagination is implemented; the handler returns every matching row in one response'

expectations.text: The missing admin permission check is identified (design says 'requires admin permission')
expectations.evidence: High finding at api/routes/admin.py:9-12: 'The handler has no admin permission check'

expectations.text: Findings are ordered by severity, highest first (no Low listed before a High)
expectations.evidence: Order in document: 4 `High` findings, then 4 `Medium`, then 1 `Low`

expectations.text: Each finding includes a file path with a line number reference, severity in backticks, Problem/Why/Source of truth/Proposed fix structure
expectations.evidence: Every finding bullet uses backtick severity, `[api/routes/admin.py:N]` location, and Problem/Why it matters/Source of truth/Proposed fix lines

expectations.text: The use of POST sub-resource verb (`/api/tasks/:id/complete`) is identified as inconsistent with REST conventions — should be PATCH on the resource
expectations.evidence: First High finding: 'State transition is modeled as `POST /api/tasks/:id/complete` — a sub-resource action verb instead of a `PATCH` on the task resource. ... Replace with `PATCH /api/tasks/:id`'

expectations.text: The missing Idempotency-Key on the side-effectful POST is identified as a finding
expectations.evidence: Second High finding: 'No `Idempotency-Key` handling on a side-effectful, non-GET request that updates state, sends an email, and writes an audit row'

expectations.text: The snake_case wire field `task_id` is identified as a finding (should be camelCase)
expectations.evidence: First Medium finding: 'Response uses `task_id` (snake_case) on the wire ... Return `{ "taskId": ..., "status": "COMPLETED" }`'

expectations.text: The `200` status (rather than reflecting the state-transition correctly) and the bypass of the canonical error envelope are noted where relevant
expectations.evidence: Medium finding addresses 200 status semantics; another Medium finding addresses missing error handling that bypasses the canonical `{ code, message, details?, requestId }` envelope

expectations.evidence: Order: 3 `High` findings, 5 `Medium` findings, 1 `Low` finding
